item.title: Who could benefit from Trump's $1.7+ billion "weaponization" fund?
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche announced the creation of the fund as part of the settlement of President Trump's lawsuit against the IRS over the leaking of his tax returns.

item.title: Judge rules some key evidence can be used in Luigi Mangione's state trial
item.description: A New York judge ruled Monday on whether evidence found in Luigi Mangione's backpack during his arrest can be used during his state trial.

item.title: The story of the 1996 shootdown that could lead to Raúl Castro's indictment
item.description: Thirty years ago, a Cuban fighter jet shot down two civilian planes operated by Florida-based exile group Brothers to the Rescue, an incident that inflamed U.S.-Cuba relations.

item.title: CBS News Radio flashback: "Big Four" hold Geneva Summit in 1955
item.description: The year is 1955, and the world is a decade removed from the Allied victory in World War II, a monstrous conflict of never-before-seen carnage and slaughter. To avoid a third world war, the great powers tried something new: a meeting to explore trade relations, peace talks and arms control. Nikole Killion introduces CBS News Radio's coverage of the Geneva Summit.

item.title: No children hurt in shooting at Islamic Center of San Diego, officials say
item.description: At least three adults were killed when a pair of gunmen opened fire at the Islamic Center of San Diego on Monday. The two teenage suspects were found dead inside a nearby car, officials say. CBS News' Anna Schecter has the latest.

item.title: Los Angeles mayoral candidate Nithya Raman acknowledges "real sense of frustration" among voters
item.description: More than a dozen candidates are challenging Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass in June's primary. While Bass holds a comfortable lead in most polls, two candidates, Spencer Pratt and Nithya Raman, have gained ground in recent months. Raman joins "The Takeout" to discuss the race and her candidacy.

item.title: American doctor working in Congo tests positive for Ebola
item.description: An American doctor working in the Congo amid an Ebola outbreak has tested positive for the virus. CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Céline Gounder has the latest.

item.title: 3 killed in shooting at Islamic Center of San Diego, police say; 2 suspects dead
item.description: Three people were killed in a shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego, and two suspected shooters were found dead inside a vehicle nearby, police said.

item.title: Musk says he'll appeal dismissal of lawsuit against OpenAI
item.description: A California jury on Monday unanimously dismissed Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman. CBS News senior business and technology correspondent Jo Ling Kent reports.

item.title: Cuba acquired drones and is discussing using them against U.S., Axios reports
item.description: In an exclusive, Axios reports that Cuba has acquired more than 300 military drones and is discussing using them to potentially attack the Guantanamo Bay U.S. detention center and possibly even part of the U.S. mainland. Axios White House correspondent Marc Caputo joins to unpack his reporting.

item.title: A timeline of the Karen Read case
item.description: A look at the timeline of events in Karen Read's high-profile Massachusetts murder trial and retrial. Read was acquitted of killing her boyfriend, Boston police officer John O'Keefe.

item.title: Trump says U.S. won't go forward with scheduled attack on Iran
item.description: President Trump posted on social media Monday afternoon that the U.S. will not go through with attacks on Iran scheduled for Tuesday, at the request of Middle East allies. CBS News political reporter Zak Hudak has more.

item.title: Primary elections to test Trump's grip on GOP
item.description: President Trump is facing a critical political test this week, with primaries in six states. CBS News' Anthony Salvanto and Aaron Navarro have more on the president's poll numbers and Rep. Thomas Massie's campaign in Kentucky. Then, Adam Beam, deputy politics editor at The Atlanta Journal-Constitution, joins to preview Georgia's primary day.

item.title: Trump settles IRS lawsuit, sets up $1.7B fund for claims of "weaponization"
item.description: President Trump had accused the Treasury Department and IRS of unlawfully allowing a government contractor to leak his tax returns and those of his sons and company.

item.title: Day 3 of LIRR strike brings no clear sign of a deal as NYC commuters cope
item.description: LIRR commuters are taking shuttle buses and subways into New York City as the largest commuter rail system in North America remains shut down.

item.title: Trump says he's called off plans for a "scheduled attack of Iran"
item.description: President Trump said he received a request from the leaders of Qatar, Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates.

item.title: Austin shooting spree: 3 held after at least 12 incidents, police say
item.description: Mayor Kirk Watson said no motive has been identified and the shootings appear to be random.

item.title: Police investigating shooting at Islamic Center of San Diego as a hate crime
item.description: At least three victims, and two suspected shooters, are dead in Monday's shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego, police say. CBS News' Samantha Vinograd and Anna Schecter have the latest.

item.title: Trump calls off attack on Iran amid "serious negotiations" toward peace deal
item.description: President Trump had warned Sunday that "the Clock is Ticking" for Iran to accept a peace agreement.

item.title: Jury unanimously dismisses Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI
item.description: California jury dismissed all charges, finding that Musk missed the three-year statute of limitations to file suit.

item.title: 3 killed in shooting at Islamic Center of San Diego; 2 suspects also dead | Special Report
item.description: Three adults and two suspects are dead after a shooting on Monday at the Islamic Center of San Diego. "CBS Evening News" anchor Tony Dokoupil leads a special report.

item.title: Pay for American workers is lagging inflation — again
item.description: U.S. wage growth is lagging inflation for the first time since 2023, eroding consumers' purchasing power.

item.title: 1 of 2 suspects dead after shooting at Islamic Center of San Diego, sources say
item.description: At least one of two shooters at a San Diego mosque is dead, sources say. Police say the threat is neutralized. CBS News' Anna Schecter reports.

item.title: American doctor working in Congo tests positive for Ebola
item.description: An American medical missionary in the Democratic Republic of the Congo has a confirmed case of Ebola, and is being taken to Germany for treatment.

item.title: Ebola strain in latest outbreak has no vaccine, no treatment
item.description: The virus behind the latest Ebola outbreak is the Bundibugyo virus, which is less common and there is no vaccine or treatment.

item.title: 198 pounds of cocaine found hidden in Kim Kardashian brand underwear
item.description: A truck driver was sentenced to over 13 years in prison for smuggling $9.4 million worth of cocaine in a shipment of Skims, Kim Kardashian's shapewear brand.

item.title: Divers find bodies of 4 Italians in Maldives sea cave
item.description: Italy's Foreign Ministry said Monday rescuers located the bodies of four Italian divers who were believed to be deep inside an underwater cave in a Maldive atoll.

item.title: The Man with Two Names
item.description: After a conviction in the arson death of a billionaire in Monaco, Ted Maher changes his name and puts out a hit on his estranged wife in New Mexico. He denies it all. "48 Hours" correspondent Erin Moriarty reports.

item.title: Joe Hunter's Mission
item.description: A "Survivor" contestant believes his sister was murdered and is determined to be her voice. "48 Hours" correspondent Natalie Morales reports.

item.title: CBS News Baltimore Sports Live
